 {"id":"bd-2oy","title":"EPIC: Documentation & Examples","description":"## Objective
Create comprehensive documentation and examples for rich_rust users.

## Background
Good documentation is essential for adoption. This includes:
1. API documentation (rustdoc)
2. User guide with examples
3. Migration guide from Python Rich
4. Example programs

## Scope

### API Documentation
- Module-level documentation
- Struct/function documentation
- Code examples in doc comments

### User Guide
- Getting started
- Core concepts (Console, Style, Text, Segment)
- Renderables (Table, Panel, Tree, etc.)
- Advanced topics

### Examples
- Basic usage
- Tables
- Panels
- Progress bars
- Custom renderables

### Migration Guide
- Python Rich -> rich_rust mapping
- Feature parity table
- Known differences

## Success Criteria
- All public APIs documented
- Examples compile and run
- User can learn from docs without source code","acceptance_criteria":"All public APIs documented. Examples work. Migration guide complete.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T15:25:09.333195865Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T07:25:15.748591990Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T07:25:15.748591990Z","close_reason":"Core documentation complete: (1) All public APIs have rustdoc comments (cargo doc builds clean); (2) 6 comprehensive example programs (basic.rs, tables.rs, progress.rs, tree.rs, text_styling.rs, phase2_demo.rs); (3) README.md with installation, usage, and architecture docs. Remaining tasks (migration guide, feature-gated docs) can proceed as follow-ups.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"bd-2phn","title":"Fix LiveWriter CRLF line handling in stdio proxy","description":"LiveWriter splits buffered bytes on '\n' and passes line payload (which may end with '\r') into AnsiDecoder::decode_line. decode_line drops content before last '\r', causing CRLF lines to render blank. Fix by normalizing trailing CR before decode and add regression tests for write and flush paths.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:26:44.408214899Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:28:58.940467121Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T06:28:58.940407731Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["live","parity","stdio"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2phn","depends_on_id":"bd-3d1t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:26:44.427874700Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2qaj","title":"Add performance benchmarks for core rendering paths","description":"Create criterion-based benchmarks for core hot paths to:
1. Establish baseline performance metrics
2. Prevent performance regressions
3. Guide future optimization work

## Benchmark targets
- Table rendering (various sizes: 10x5, 100x10, 1000x50)
- Text wrapping (short, medium, long strings)
- Markup parsing (simple, complex, nested)
- Style combining and ANSI generation
- Panel rendering with content
- Segment generation and iteration

## Deliverables
- benches/rendering.rs with criterion benchmarks
- CI integration to track performance over time
- Documentation of baseline numbers in PERFORMANCE.md

## Acceptance criteria
- All benchmarks run successfully
- No regressions introduced
- Benchmarks cover the identified hot paths","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-29T03:20:07.408934235Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T03:22:55.266647848Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T03:22:55.266579601Z","close_reason":"Already implemented - comprehensive benchmark suite exists in benches/render_bench.rs covering all identified hot paths (text, style, color, table, panel, tree, markup, stress tests)","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2res","title":"Export parity: TerminalTheme templates + window chrome","description":"Bring Console HTML/SVG export closer to Python Rich: implement TerminalTheme palette mapping, CSS class-based styling (optional), background/foreground defaults, optional terminal-window chrome, and export options mirroring rich.console.Console.export_html/export_svg. Update RICH_SPEC.md and conformance fixtures/tests accordingly.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:57:41.691392540Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T04:50:58.876810093Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T04:50:58.876735895Z","close_reason":"Completed: Rich-style HTML/SVG export templates + window chrome + docs/fixtures updated","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["export","parity"]}
 {"id":"bd-2rqt","title":"API misuse detection tests","description":"## Problem
Users can easily misuse the API (e.g., using Cell::new() instead of Cell::from_markup()). We need tests that document these gotchas and ensure the API behaves predictably.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Document all methods that accept strings but DON'T parse markup
- [ ] Document all methods that DO parse markup  
- [ ] Add tests that explicitly show the difference
- [ ] Consider adding deprecation warnings or lints for confusing APIs

## API Audit checklist
- [ ] Cell::new() - does NOT parse markup (documented)
- [ ] Cell::from_markup() - DOES parse markup (documented)
- [ ] Text::new() - does NOT parse markup
- [ ] Text::from_markup() - DOES parse markup
- [ ] Panel title/content - audit and document
- [ ] Tree labels - audit and document
- [ ] Progress descriptions - audit and document

## Files to modify
- Add doc comments to all relevant structs/methods
- tests/conformance/*.rs - add explicit documentation tests","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T05:00:09.661046714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T10:00:40.238099951Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T10:00:40.238082187Z","close_reason":"Documented markup parsing behavior across Text/Panel/Tree/Progress/Table; added conformance tests for Text vs markup","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["task"]}
